name: White Pan Bread
recipeIngredient: -- (up to 5 additional cups of flour as needed)
5 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted -- or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Oil two (8 1/2 by 4 1/2 by 2 1/2-inch) loaf pans.
2. Whisk yeast into warm water and set aside while preparing other ingredients.
3. To mix dough by hand:
a. Place 5 cups flour, salt, and sugar in a mixing bowl.
b. Stir in the yeast mixture and melted butter.
c. Continue to stir until a rough dough forms.
d. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured work surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes. If the dough is excessively soft and sticky, add remaining flour one tablespoon at a time.
4. To mix dough in a heavy-duty mixer:
a. Place flour, salt, and sugar in a mixer bowl.
b. Add yeast mixture and melted butter.
c. Mix on low speed with a dough hook for about 5 minutes, or until the dough is smooth and elastic.
d. If the dough is excessively soft and sticky, add remaining flour one tablespoon at a time.
5. To mix dough in a food processor:
a. Place flour, salt, and sugar in a bowl fitted with a metal blade.
b. Pulse several times to mix.
c. Add yeast mixture and butter and pulse 8 to 10 times until the dough forms a ball.
d. If the dough is excessively soft and sticky, add remaining flour one tablespoon at a time, and pulse, until the dough forms a coherent ball.
6. Let the dough rest for 5 minutes, then run the machine continuously for 30 seconds.
7. Place the dough in an oiled bowl and turn to oil all surfaces. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and allow it to rise until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
8. Turn risen dough out onto a lightly floured work surface (you may need the help of a scraper). Deflate the dough and divide into two equal pieces.
9. To form a loaf:
a. Make sure the surface is free of excess flour.
b. Stretch the dough into a rectangle.
c. Fold in the short ends of dough until it is approximately the length of the pan.
d. Fold the far long edge over to the middle.
e. Fold over the other long side and compress to form a tight cylinder.
f. Place the loaf in the pan, seam side down.
g. Cover the pan with plastic wrap.
10. Repeat with the second piece of dough. Allow to rise until doubled.
11. When loaves are almost doubled, preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and set the rack at middle level.
12. When loaves are completely risen, place them in the oven and bake for 30 minutes or until the loaves are golden brown and firm and the internal temperature is about 210°F (100°C).
13. Unmold loaves to a rack and cool on their sides.
